commands.review_summary:
The PR adds terminal-progress stall classification for diagnostic recovery, exposes Codex runtime auth routes in models status output, and shows runtime-policy session aliases in sessions output.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is clear for the intended branches: the diff adds focused tests and the current main recovery/session/auth helpers show the path. I did not run tests or live recovery proof because this review is read-only and must not create artifacts in the checkout.
commands.review_followup: Human review should decide whether the incomplete recovery proof and overlap with https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82733 are acceptable before merge; there is no narrow automated repair to apply.

target.title: fix: harden release stability diagnostics and session visibility
target.body:
## Why

This is a narrow release-stability bundle for the current OpenClaw mainline. It avoids any broader Codex-harness migration and only carries the pieces that help operators diagnose or recover known unstable release paths.

The changes are intentionally small:

- classify a queued session behind terminal embedded progress as stalled so recovery can run after the active-abort threshold
- show Codex runtime auth routes in `models list --status --json` and the human status output so synthetic OpenAI Codex auth is visible instead of looking missing
- expose the runtime-policy per-DM session alias in `sessions --json` and the session flags column when a direct external chat is intentionally collapsed onto `agent:main:main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: release-stability operators need to see the derived runtime-policy per-DM session key for collapsed external DMs, and need `models status --json` to explain Codex runtime auth routes instead of only reporting the canonical provider as missing.

Real environment tested: real OpenClaw CLI entrypoint from this checkout, `OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (7e10b2a)`, on local macOS with an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/openclaw-release-stability-state` so the machine's existing `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` did not affect the result.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/openclaw-release-stability-state \
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 \
pnpm openclaw sessions --json \
  --store /Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/openclaw-release-stability-sessions-proof.json \
  --limit 1
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "path": "/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/openclaw-release-stability-sessions-proof.json",
  "count": 1,
  "sessions": [
    {
      "key": "agent:main:main",
      "sessionId": "release-stability-proof",
      "agentRuntime": {
        "id": "codex",
        "source": "implicit"
      },
      "runtimePolicySessionKey": "agent:main:telegram:default:direct:42"
    }
  ]
}
```

```bash
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/openclaw-release-stability-state \
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 \
pnpm openclaw models status --json
```

```json
{
  "auth": {
    "missingProvidersInUse": ["openai"],
    "runtimeAuthRoutes": [
      {
        "provider": "openai",
        "runtime": "codex",
        "authProvider": "openai-codex",
        "status": "missing",
        "effective": {
          "kind": "missing",
          "detail": "missing"
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: `sessions --json` returned the persisted row key `agent:main:main` plus `runtimePolicySessionKey: "agent:main:telegram:default:direct:42"`; `models status --json` returned `auth.runtimeAuthRoutes[0]` showing `provider: "openai"`, `runtime: "codex"`, `authProvider: "openai-codex"`, and `status: "missing"` for the isolated no-auth state.

Not tested: no live Telegram message was sent and no LCM behavior was exercised in this PR; LCM is intentionally out of scope.

## Issue Links

- Fixes / complements #71127. This complements #82733 rather than replacing it: #82733 handles stale/idle embedded lanes, while this handles queued work stuck behind terminal-looking embedded progress.
- References #83484 by exposing the runtime-policy session alias without changing Telegram routing or session storage.
- References #83485. The core false-missing-auth behavior was addressed by #83033; this adds the missing operator-facing route explanation.

LCM is deliberately out of scope here because the LCM-side fix is already tracked separately in `lossless-claw`.

## What Changed

- `src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.ts`
  - exports terminal progress detection
  - treats queued work behind terminal embedded progress as `session.stalled`

- `src/logging/diagnostic.ts`
  - reuses the shared terminal-progress classifier
  - allows the existing heartbeat recovery path to recover queued sessions once the active-abort threshold is reached

- `src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts`
  - adds `auth.runtimeAuthRoutes` to JSON output
  - adds a human `Runtime auth` section for Codex-backed OpenAI text routes

- `src/commands/sessions.ts` and `src/commands/sessions-table.ts`
  - derives and displays a `runtimePolicySessionKey` alias for collapsed external direct sessions
  - keeps the persisted row key unchanged

## Non-Goals

- no LCM changes
- no Telegram routing rewrite
- no full Codex-harness migration
- no change to how session rows are persisted

## Validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/sessions.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.logging.config.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts src/commands/sessions-table.ts src/commands/sessions.test.ts src/commands/sessions.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic.ts`
- `git diff --check`
